acceptOwnership().details: The new owner accepts the ownership transfer.

acknowledgeInvoice(uint256).details: After `editInvoice` is called, `payerAcknowledged` is set to false         and `payPrepaidInvoice` will revert until the payer calls this function.         Only the invoice's assigned payer may call it, and only while PENDING.

addSupportedToken(address,uint8).details: address(0) is intentionally blocked here. Native ETH is already         whitelisted in the constructor and is handled exclusively through         `depositETH` / `withdrawETH`. Allowing address(0) through this path         would risk overwriting the ETH decimal entry and causing confusion.         To re-enable ETH after an accidental `removeSupportedToken(address(0))`         call, have the admin call `supportedTokens` setter or redeploy.         In practice, ETH should never be removed.

params.decimals: Decimal precision of the token (e.g. 6 for USDT / USDC,                 18 for WETH). Must be provided by admin; the contract does                 not call `decimals()` on the token to avoid dependency on                 IERC20Metadata and potential non-standard implementations.

adminRefundToPayer(uint256,uint256).details: If `refundAmount` equals the full escrow: full refund, no fee.         If `refundAmount` is less: the remainder is released to the merchant         after deducting the platform fee on the remainder only.

params.invoiceId: Invoice whose escrow is to be (partially) refunded.
params.refundAmount: Amount to return to the payer (≤ escrow amount).

params.invoiceId: Invoice whose escrow is to be released to merchant.

params.maxPerCycle: Maximum amount that can be deducted per cycle.
params.merchant: Merchant address allowed to trigger pulls.
params.totalLimit: Hard cap across all cycles combined (0 = unlimited).

params.token: Token address; use address(0) for ETH.

returns._0: True if permission has been granted.

cancelInvoice(uint256,string).details: Rules:         • Merchant   → can cancel their own PENDING invoice only.         • Admin / Employee → can cancel any PENDING or ACTIVE invoice.         Invoices in ACTIVE state are mid-stream recurring invoices; only         privileged roles can abort them to protect in-progress work.

params.evidence: Text or reference supporting the challenge.

params.invoiceId: Invoice in AWAITING_CONFIRMATION state past the confirmation deadline.

params.defaultFeeBps: Initial global fee in basis points (e.g. 250 = 2.5 %).

params.amount: Gross invoice amount in token base units.
params.dueDate: Overall expiry timestamp; no payments accepted after this.
params.isRecurring: Whether this invoice repeats on an interval.
params.maxCycles: Maximum number of cycles to bill (0 if non-recurring).
params.recurringInterval: Seconds between billing cycles (0 if non-recurring).
params.token: Payment token (USDT, USDC, or address(0) for ETH).

depositETH().details: The ETH is held by the contract; only the caller's internal ledger         balance is updated. No ETH leaves the contract until the user         explicitly calls `withdrawETH`.

depositToken(address,uint256).details: Uses `safeTransferFrom`; credits only the amount actually received         so that fee-on-transfer tokens are handled correctly.

params.amount: Amount to deposit in token base units.
params.token: Token address (must be whitelisted; not address(0) — use depositETH).

params.newAmount: New gross amount in token base units (0 = keep current).
params.newDueDate: New overall expiry timestamp (0 = keep current).
params.newMaxCycles: New maximum number of cycles (0 = keep current).
params.newRecurringInterval: New cycle interval in seconds (0 = keep current).

emergencyWithdrawAll(address[],address[].details: Two separate loops handle different fund pools:         Ledger loop — iterates `users × tokens` and sends each user's         internal ledger balance directly to their wallet.         Escrow loop — iterates `escrowInvoiceIds`; for each unreleased         escrow record the full amount is refunded to the original payer.         If an ETH send fails, the amount is credited back to the payer's         ledger so the user can withdraw it manually once unpaused (or in         a second emergency call). ERC-20 failures bubble up via SafeERC20.         To avoid unbounded loops the caller supplies both lists. Iterate         in batches if the user base is very large.

params.escrowInvoiceIds: List of invoice IDs that have active (unreleased)                        escrow records to be refunded to the payer.
params.tokens: List of token addresses to process per user                        (include address(0) for ETH).
params.users: List of user wallet addresses whose ledger balances                        should be swept.

finalizeResolution(uint256).details: Callable by anyone after the deadline — the outcome is immutable         at that point and no privileged role is required.

params.invoiceId: Invoice in CHALLENGE_PENDING state whose deadline has passed.

returns.isUserOverride: True if a per-user override is active.
returns.value: Fee value (bps if PERCENTAGE; 0 if FLAT — see flat                       fee lookups via `getEffectiveFlatFee`).

getEffectiveFlatFee(address,address).details: Returns 0 if the active fee mode is PERCENTAGE (not applicable).

returns.amount: Flat fee in token base units.

returns.frozen: True if a dispute is blocking release.
returns.released: True if escrow has already been settled.

getPayerApprovedMerchants(address).details: Callers should cross-reference with `getRecurringApproval` to         determine which approvals are still active.

returns._0: Array of merchant addresses (insertion order, deduplicated per              payer-merchant pair via `_payerMerchantTracked`).

returns.active: Whether the approval is currently active.
returns.totalSpent: Cumulative amount deducted under this approval.

returns.decimals: Decimal precision stored at whitelist time.

returns._0: UserConfig (employee flag, merchant flag, subscription state).

markComplete(uint256).details: Only valid on PAID status before the invoice dueDate. Once dueDate         passes, the payer may call reclaimFunds() instead (M-3).

params.invoiceId: Prepaid invoice to mark as complete.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

payPostpaidInvoice(uint256,uint256).details: No escrow is created; the fee is deducted immediately and the net         amount is credited to the merchant's internal ledger. Invoice moves         directly to COMPLETED.

payPrepaidInvoice(uint256,uint256).details: No platform fee is taken at this stage; the fee is deducted when         the payer calls `confirmCompletion`, the merchant calls `claimPayment`         after the confirmation window expires, or on dispute resolution.         A `deadline` timestamp prevents stale signed payment intents.

pendingOwner().details: Returns the address of the pending owner.

previewFee(address,uint256,address).details: Passes the token address to `_calculateFee` so that flat-fee         lookups return the correct per-token amount rather than a generic         value that would be meaningless across tokens with different decimals.

params.amount: Hypothetical gross payment amount in token base units.
params.merchant: Merchant address (determines which fee tier applies).
params.token: Token that would be used for payment.

raiseDispute(uint256,string).details: Only valid on AWAITING_CONFIRMATION. The payer cannot dispute while         still on PAID status — they must wait for the merchant to signal         completion first, or use reclaimFunds() once the dueDate has passed.

reclaimFunds(uint256).details: Only valid on PAID status after dueDate. Once the merchant calls         markComplete() the status moves to AWAITING_CONFIRMATION and this         function is no longer available.

params.invoiceId: Prepaid invoice in PAID state past its dueDate.

params.externalWallet: External wallet to associate (not zero, not self).

removeSupportedToken(address).details: address(0) / NATIVE_ETH is explicitly blocked. Delisting ETH would         prevent new ETH-denominated invoices and recurring approvals from         being created (both gate on `onlySupportedToken`). Deposits and         withdrawals of ETH would still work because they do not check         `supportedTokens`, but the broken invoice path is enough reason to         guard this. ETH should never be delisted; redeploy if needed.

removeUserFee(address).details: Any per-token flat fee entries remain in storage but are ignored         while `isSet == false`.

params.releaseToMerchant: true  → release net amount to merchant.                          false → full refund to payer (no fee charged).

params.merchant: Merchant whose approval is being revoked.
params.token: Token for which approval is revoked.

params.duration: New window length in seconds (e.g. 7 days, 30 days).

setDefaultFee(uint8,uint256).details: For FLAT mode: calling this sets `defaultFeeConfig.feeType = FLAT`         but the `value` field is ignored. Call `setDefaultFlatFee` per token         to configure the actual per-token flat amounts.         For PERCENTAGE mode: `value` is in basis points (max 10 000).

params.value: Basis points for PERCENTAGE; ignored for FLAT.

setDefaultFlatFee(address,uint256).details: Store amounts in the token's own base units:           USDT / USDC (6 decimals): 1_000_000 = 1 token           ETH (18 decimals):        5e14 ≈ 0.0005 ETH         This avoids any cross-decimal arithmetic inside the contract.

params.amount: Flat fee in token base units (0 = no fee for this token).

params.value: true = allow withdrawal; false = block withdrawal.

params.limit: New monthly limit (0 = always charge a fee on family transfers).

params.max: New cap (0 = no challenges allowed after the first ruling).

setTierDiscount(uint8,uint256).details: Example: base fee = 100 tokens, GOLD discount = 2 000 bps →         reduction = 100 × 2000 / 10000 = 20 → effective fee = 80 tokens.

params.discountBps: Discount in basis points (max 10 000 = 100% of the fee).

params.feeType: PERCENTAGE or FLAT. For FLAT, also call `setUserFlatFee`.
params.value: Basis points for PERCENTAGE; ignored for FLAT.

setUserFlatFee(address,address,uint256).details: Multiple tokens can each have their own flat amount for the same         merchant. Each call only updates the slot for the given token, so         calling once for USDT and once for ETH gives independent amounts.         Example for a merchant billed 0.5 USDT flat on every invoice:           setUserFlatFee(merchant, usdtAddress, 500_000)

params.amount: Flat fee in token base units.

params.tier: New tier (STANDARD, SILVER, GOLD, or PLATINUM).

returns._0: Invoice counter (equals the last-issued invoice ID).

transferOwnership(address).details: Starts the ownership transfer of the contract to a new account. Replaces the pending transfer if there is one. Can only be called by the current owner. Setting `newOwner` to the zero address is allowed; this can be used to cancel an initiated ownership transfer.

transferToUser(address,address,uint256,b.details: Fee is computed using the global default fee config only (no per-user         overrides, no tier discounts) via `_calculateDefaultFee`. The monthly         receive count is keyed to the *recipient* using a calendar month key         (YYYYMM) and is always incremented for family transfers regardless of         whether the fee-free threshold has been reached.

params.amount: Gross amount to deduct from the caller's ledger.
params.isFamilyTransfer: When true: treated as a family / friends transfer.                         No fee is charged if the recipient has not yet reached                         their monthly free-receive limit; the count is always                         incremented for tracking.

triggerRecurring(uint256).details: Checks (in order):         1. Caller is the invoice merchant.         2. Invoice is recurring.         3. Invoice status is PENDING (first cycle) or ACTIVE (subsequent).         4. Max cycles not yet reached.         5. Current time is past `nextDueDate`.         6. Current time is before the invoice's overall `dueDate`.         7. Payer has an active approval covering this merchant + token.         8. Payer approval limits are not exceeded.         9. Payer has sufficient ledger balance.

withdrawETH(uint256).details: Follows checks-effects-interactions: ledger is decremented before         the external call to prevent reentrancy.

params.amount: Amount of ETH to withdraw, in wei.

withdrawToken(address,uint256).details: Intentionally does NOT require the token to be currently whitelisted.         If admin delists a token, existing holders must still be able to         withdraw their balance. Gating on `onlySupportedToken` here would         permanently trap funds for any user who held a delisted token.

params.amount: Amount to withdraw in token base units.

BPS_DENOMINATOR().notice: Denominator for percentage fees expressed in basis points.         100 bps = 1 %, 10 000 bps = 100 %.

NATIVE_ETH().notice: Sentinel used in the internal ledger to represent native ETH.

VERSION().notice: Contract version — increment on each deployment.

acknowledgeInvoice(uint256).notice: Payer acknowledges an edited invoice, re-enabling payment.

addEmployee(address).notice: Admin grants the employee role to a wallet.         Employees can resolve disputes and update per-user fees.

addSupportedToken(address,uint8).notice: Admin adds a new token to the supported whitelist and stores its         decimal precision for frontend consumers.

adminRefundToPayer(uint256,uint256).notice: Admin or employee refunds some or all of the escrowed amount to         the payer. Supports both full and partial refunds.

adminReleaseToMerchant(uint256).notice: Admin or employee force-releases escrow to the merchant outside         the formal dispute flow (e.g. after off-chain resolution).

approveFamilySender(address,bool).notice: Recipient opts a sender in (or out) as an approved family sender,         enabling fee-free transfers from that sender up to the monthly limit.

approveRecurring(address,address,uint256.notice: Payer pre-authorises the merchant to pull recurring payments for         a specific token.

balanceOf(address,address).notice: Returns the internal ledger balance for a user and token.

canWithdrawExternal(address).notice: Returns whether a user holds admin-granted external-withdrawal permission.

challengeDispute(uint256,string).notice: Payer contests a merchant-wins dispute ruling while the challenge         window is still open. Resets the invoice to DISPUTED status so the         admin or employee can re-examine the case.

challengeWindowDuration().notice: Duration (seconds) of the payer challenge window after a         merchant-wins dispute ruling. Default: 30 days.

claimPayment(uint256).notice: Merchant claims payment after the payer's confirmation window has         expired without a confirmCompletion() or raiseDispute() call.

confirmCompletion(uint256).notice: Payer confirms that work is satisfactory, releasing escrowed funds         to the merchant after deducting the platform fee.

confirmationWindow().notice: How long (seconds) the payer has to confirm or dispute after         the merchant calls markComplete(). Default: 7 days.

constructor.notice: Deploys the platform and sets the default percentage-based fee.         No tokens are whitelisted at deploy time — call addSupportedToken()         after deployment to enable each payment token.

createInvoice(address,address,uint256,ui.notice: Merchant creates a new invoice. Status starts as PENDING.

defaultFeeConfig().notice: Platform-wide default fee (mode + bps value for PERCENTAGE).

depositETH().notice: Deposit native ETH into the pool vault.

depositToken(address,uint256).notice: Deposit an ERC-20 token into the pool vault.

editInvoice(uint256,uint256,uint256,stri.notice: Merchant edits a PENDING invoice before the payer has paid.         Non-recurring fields (`amount`, `dueDate`, `description`) are always         editable. `recurringInterval` and `maxCycles` may only be changed on         recurring invoices. Pass 0 / empty string for fields you do not wish         to update.

emergencyWithdrawAll(address[],address[].notice: Emergency: admin returns all user balances AND unresolved escrow         funds back to their rightful owners. Only callable when paused.

finalizeResolution(uint256).notice: Finalises a merchant-wins ruling once the payer challenge window         has expired without a challenge. Releases escrowed funds to the         merchant after deducting the platform fee.

freeReceiveLimit().notice: Maximum fee-free family transfers a wallet may receive per 30-day         window. Admin-configurable; default 5.

getConfirmationDeadline(uint256).notice: Returns the timestamp by which the payer must confirm or dispute         after the merchant calls `markComplete()`. Returns 0 if the invoice         has not yet entered the AWAITING_CONFIRMATION state.

getEffectiveFee(address).notice: Returns the effective fee configuration for a given merchant.

getEffectiveFlatFee(address,address).notice: Returns the effective flat fee for a merchant–token pair.

getEscrow(uint256).notice: Returns escrow details for a prepaid invoice.

getExternalWallet(address).notice: Returns the external wallet registered for a given user.

getInvoice(uint256).notice: Returns the full Invoice struct for a given invoice ID.

getMerchantInvoices(address).notice: Returns all invoice IDs that a merchant has created.

getMonthlyReceiveCount(address).notice: Returns how many family transfers a wallet has received in the         current 30-day window.

getPayerApprovedMerchants(address).notice: Returns the list of merchant addresses that a payer has ever         approved for recurring payments (including revoked approvals).

getPayerInvoices(address).notice: Returns all invoice IDs assigned to a payer.

getRecurringApproval(address,address,add.notice: Returns the recurring approval between a payer, merchant, and token.

getTokenInfo(address).notice: Returns token whitelist status and decimal precision.

getUserConfig(address).notice: Returns the user configuration struct for any wallet.

getUserTier(address).notice: Returns the current loyalty tier for a given user.

isEmployee(address).notice: Quick O(1) employee check used by the `onlyAdminOrEmployee` modifier.

markComplete(uint256).notice: Merchant signals that work is done. Moves the invoice to         AWAITING_CONFIRMATION and starts the payer confirmation window.         Funds are NOT released yet — the payer must either call         confirmCompletion() or raiseDispute() within the window, or the         merchant can call claimPayment() after the window expires.

maxChallengesPerInvoice().notice: Maximum times a payer may challenge a single invoice's ruling.         Admin-configurable; default 1.

pause().notice: Admin pauses the entire contract (emergency brake).         All state-changing functions are blocked while paused.

payPostpaidInvoice(uint256,uint256).notice: Payer settles a POSTPAID invoice in a single step.

payPrepaidInvoice(uint256,uint256).notice: Payer pays a PREPAID invoice, locking the full amount in escrow.

previewFee(address,uint256,address).notice: Simulates the fee split for a hypothetical payment without         executing it. Useful for frontend quoting.

raiseDispute(uint256,string).notice: Payer raises a dispute after the merchant has called markComplete()         but before the payer has confirmed or the confirmation window has         expired. Freezes the escrow so neither party can access the funds.

reclaimFunds(uint256).notice: Payer reclaims their locked escrow when the merchant never called         markComplete() and the invoice dueDate has passed.

registerExternalWallet(address).notice: Registers an external wallet for the caller. Once registered, all         calls to `withdrawETH` / `withdrawToken` require admin-granted         external-withdrawal permission before they succeed.

rejectInvoice(uint256,string).notice: Payer rejects a PENDING invoice before making any payment.

removeEmployee(address).notice: Admin revokes the employee role from a wallet.

removeExternalWallet().notice: Removes the caller's registered external wallet, restoring         unrestricted withdrawal access.

removeSupportedToken(address).notice: Admin removes a token from the supported whitelist.         Existing ledger balances in that token are unaffected; holders         can still withdraw via `withdrawToken` even after delisting.

removeUserFee(address).notice: Admin or employee removes a per-user fee override, reverting the         merchant to the global default fee.

renounceOwnership().notice: Disabled — renouncing would permanently brick admin functions         and trap all future fee income in address(0).

resolveDispute(uint256,bool,string).notice: Admin or employee resolves an open dispute, directing escrowed         funds to the winning party.

revokeRecurring(address,address).notice: Payer revokes a previously granted recurring approval.         Any cycle not yet triggered is immediately blocked.

setChallengeWindow(uint256).notice: Admin sets the duration of the payer challenge window.

setConfirmationWindow(uint256).notice: Admin sets the confirmation window duration — how long the payer         has to call confirmCompletion() or raiseDispute() after the merchant         calls markComplete(). Default: 7 days. Minimum: 1 day.

setDefaultFee(uint8,uint256).notice: Admin switches the global fee mode and sets the percentage value.

setDefaultFlatFee(address,uint256).notice: Admin sets the global flat fee for a specific token.         Also switches the global fee mode to FLAT if not already set.

setExternalWithdrawPermission(address,bo.notice: Admin grants or revokes external-withdrawal permission for a user.         Only effective for users who have a registered external wallet.

setFreeReceiveLimit(uint256).notice: Admin sets the maximum number of fee-free family transfers a wallet         may receive per 30-day window.

setMaxChallenges(uint256).notice: Admin sets the maximum number of times a payer may challenge a         single invoice's dispute ruling (default 1).

setTierDiscount(uint8,uint256).notice: Admin sets the fee discount for a specific tier.         The discount is expressed in basis points and is applied to the         computed base fee (not the invoice amount).

setUserFee(address,uint8,uint256).notice: Admin or employee sets a custom fee tier for a specific merchant         (PERCENTAGE mode), overriding the global default for that wallet.

setUserFlatFee(address,address,uint256).notice: Admin or employee sets a per-token flat fee override for a specific         merchant. Automatically marks that merchant's fee mode as FLAT.

setUserTier(address,uint8).notice: Admin or employee assigns a loyalty tier to a user. The tier         determines the fee discount applied when the user acts as a merchant         and has no per-user fee override configured.

supportedTokens(address).notice: Whether a token is accepted by the platform.

tokenDecimals(address).notice: Decimal precision of each whitelisted token.

totalInvoices().notice: Returns the total number of invoices ever created.

transferToUser(address,address,uint256,b.notice: Transfers an internal ledger balance from the caller to another user.         Platform fee applies; the `isFamilyTransfer` flag enables fee-free         transfers up to the recipient's monthly `freeReceiveLimit`.

triggerRecurring(uint256).notice: Merchant triggers the next billing cycle on a recurring invoice.

unpause().notice: Admin unpauses the contract after resolving an emergency.

withdrawETH(uint256).notice: Withdraw native ETH from the internal ledger to the caller's wallet.

withdrawToken(address,uint256).notice: Withdraw an ERC-20 token from the internal ledger to the caller's wallet.
